title: Amr Ibn Al-Aas Mosque
description: The second mosque built in Egypt (642 AD). Contains Kufic inscriptions and Roman columns reused in construction.

title: Al-Maain Mosque
description: Mamluk era mosque known for its high minaret and massive construction, dating to the time of Sultan Al-Zahir Baybars.

title: Al-Matbouli Mosque
description: Built near the Qaitbay era in honor of Sheikh Ibrahim Al-Matbouli. Consider a masterpiece of Mamluk architecture.

title: Al-Bahr Mosque
description: A prominent mosque on the Nile bank, originally Ottoman, renovated extensively. Famous for its Andalusian-style decoration.

title: Al-Hadeedi Mosque
description: An ancient mosque in Faraskur city, one of the oldest in the governorate.

title: Tabiet Orabi (Ezbet El-Borg Fortress)
description: Defensive fortifications at the Nile mouth, expanded during the Urabi Revolt to defend against British naval attacks.

title: Old Damietta Bridge (Imbaba Bridge)
description: Historical iron bridge originally in Imbaba (Cairo), moved to Damietta. A marvel of 19th-century civil engineering.

title: Tell El-Baramoun
description: The site of Ancient Diospolis Inferior. Contains remains of a temple enclosure and Greco-Roman settlement.

title: Tell El-Deir
description: A massive archaeological mound in New Damietta containing a necropolis with limestone and terra-cotta coffins.

title: Tell El-Ghardqa
description: An archaeological island/tell in Lake Manzala known for Roman baths and domestic ruins.

title: Church of St. George
description: Greek Orthodox church containing icons dating back centuries, reflecting the cosmopolitan history of Damietta.

title: Church of the Virgin Mary
description: The main Coptic Orthodox church in Damietta, holding historical significance for the local community.

title: Sheikh Ali Al-Sayyad Mosque
description: Mosque and shrine of the famous Sufi saint Ali Al-Sayyad, attracting visitors from across the Delta.

title: Zawiyat Al-Radwan
description: Small Mamluk-era mosque known for its intricate wood carving and stucco work.

title: Abu Al-Maati Mosque
description: Located near the old cemetery, known for its large courtyard and spiritual atmosphere.

title: Tell Ezzbet El-Asfr
description: Archaeological site revealing domestic structures and pottery from the late antique period.

title: Shata Ancient Village
description: Historical village known for its salt production and the shrine of Sheikh Shata, a companion of the Arab conquerors.

title: Tell Al-Dhahab
description: Located on the east bank of the Nile branch, containing remains of ancient settlements.

title: St. Abanoub Church
description: Modern church with historical roots dedicated to the child martyr St. Abanoub.

title: Al-Mufi Mosque
description: A historic mosque with a distinct minaret style found in the old quarters.

title: Tell El-Raswa
description: Site near the outlet of the Nile, strategic location for ancient surveillance.

title: Dome of Al-Dayasit
description: Historic mausoleum dome typical of the Delta Islamic architecture.

title: Ras El-Bar Old Lighthouse
description: Historic structure marking the meeting point of the Nile and the Mediterranean.

title: Sidi Ibrahim Al-Desouki Shrine (Branch)
description: A local maqam dedicated to the famous saint, serving as a center for local Sufi gatherings.

title: Roman Cisterns of New Damietta
description: Underground water storage systems discovered during the construction of the new city.
